jokes.text: I brought a few things just to make the place look a little less short-term corporate housing.
jokes.setup: Annie being helpful with decorating
jokes.punchline: Brutal but accurate description of Jeff's apartment as 'short-term corporate housing'
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-driven burn disguised as helpfulness. Perfect Annie voice - enthusiastic yet cutting.

jokes.text: Oh, well, mi casa es su art project.
jokes.setup: Annie's criticism of his apartment
jokes.punchline: Clever twist on 'mi casa es su casa' showing Jeff's defensive wit
jokes.explanation: Economical wordplay that's both defensive and clever. Classic Jeff comeback.

jokes.text: Just a few things to add a little Annie.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining her decorating
jokes.punchline: Using her own name as a descriptor/aesthetic choice
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor. Shows Annie's self-awareness but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Sha. Sha-na-not.
jokes.setup: Jeff's accusation about playing house
jokes.punchline: Annie's flustered nonsensical denial
jokes.explanation: Shows Annie's flustered state but the wordplay is forced and not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Let's just live with them for a night. We can totally return them. Oh, let's totally return them.
jokes.setup: Annie suggesting they keep the curtains
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately echoing her return suggestion with obvious eagerness
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's immediate desire to undo Annie's changes through quick agreement on returning them.

jokes.text: You realize you've just put your friends in a very awkward position. A gift creates obligation.
jokes.setup: Annie bringing a gift despite no-gifts rule
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical but accurate analysis of gift psychology
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - turning social nicety into uncomfortable truth. Very character-appropriate cynicism.

jokes.text: Oh, who couldn't have seen that coming?
jokes.setup: Second person arrives with a gift after Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic predictability observation
jokes.explanation: Predictable sarcasm. Shows Jeff's cynicism but isn't particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: There's a real problem with resistance in this group.
jokes.setup: Multiple people saying they 'couldn't resist' bringing gifts
jokes.punchline: Jeff's meta-observation about the group's lack of self-control
jokes.explanation: Good meta-observation that acknowledges the pattern while being character-appropriate for Jeff.

jokes.text: Oh, Annie explained to me that 'no gifts' means 'bring gifts.' I'm getting it. No means yes.
jokes.setup: Abed learning Christmas social rules from Annie
jokes.punchline: Accidentally landing on a disturbing misinterpretation of consent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy through Abed's literal interpretation. The innocent delivery makes the disturbing implication even funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I was hoping we could do a Die Hard in a restaurant. There's a place called Nakatomi Sushi. I hear the Hans Grouper is to die for.
jokes.setup: Troy wanting to do Die Hard themed Christmas
jokes.punchline: Restaurant name pun on Nakatomi Plaza and fish pun on Hans Gruber
jokes.explanation: Clever double pun that shows Troy's pop culture obsession. Well-constructed reference humor.

jokes.text: I'll bet she does.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning Kendra does 'assistant work'
jokes.punchline: Jeff's suggestive interpretation
jokes.explanation: Predictable innuendo. Shows Jeff's character but is low-effort humor.

jokes.text: But, for the purpose of story symmetry, I suggest we wait here silently until the Professor arrives. It's the only real plot point we need next.
jokes.setup: Group planning their strategy
jokes.punchline: Abed breaking fourth wall to acknowledge narrative structure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-commentary. Acknowledges the show's structure while moving the plot forward. Highly character-integrated.

jokes.text: No. Nor was anyone else.
jokes.setup: Professor asking if he was expected to bring a gift
jokes.punchline: Jeff's pointed response emphasizing the no-gifts rule everyone ignored
jokes.explanation: Jeff's sarcastic emphasis on the rule everyone broke. Good callback to earlier frustration.

jokes.text: Did you hear him use 'feminine' as an insult? Typical phallo-centric worldview.
jokes.setup: Professor describing apartment as 'very feminine'
jokes.punchline: Britta's over-analytical feminist interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta - turning casual comment into academic criticism. Very character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Yippee-ki-yay, Father Christmas. I tried to get you 'taking off shoes,' but I didn't know how to wrap it.
jokes.setup: Troy giving Abed a Die Hard themed gift
jokes.punchline: Christmas version of Die Hard catchphrase and absurd gift description
jokes.explanation: Creative callback with good wordplay. Shows Troy's thoughtfulness and pop culture obsession.

jokes.text: Don't you mix it with cherry pop or Monterey Jack cheese?
jokes.setup: Professor commenting on serving scotch neat in America
jokes.punchline: Absurdly wrong stereotypes about American drinking habits
jokes.explanation: Shows Professor's condescending attitude through absurd American stereotypes. Character-appropriate snobbery.

jokes.text: Actually, you know, I was talking to the ladies.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to engage Professor about cuisine/history
jokes.punchline: Professor dismissing Jeff to focus on women
jokes.explanation: Creates awkward moment and establishes Professor's sexist character. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: He called me Broken Barbie all year.
jokes.setup: Britta explaining why Professor hates her
jokes.punchline: Revealing the cruel but apt nickname
jokes.explanation: Brutal but accurate nickname that captures both Britta's appearance and dysfunction. Economical and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Well, there was a secret Tom Waits show in town, so I did the math.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he blew off his section
jokes.punchline: Treating concert attendance as mathematical calculation
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's selfish calculation and rationalization. Character-appropriate prioritization of personal desires.

jokes.text: I copied and pasted the lyrics to war, what is it good for? Good God.
jokes.setup: Jeff admitting he didn't leave section blank
jokes.punchline: Revealing he submitted song lyrics as historical analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff move - lazy but with a twist. The song choice is thematically appropriate to history paper, making it both absurd and clever.

jokes.text: I have carefully cultivated a persona that screams, 'you're on your own.'
jokes.setup: Britta criticizing Jeff for letting friends down
jokes.punchline: Jeff's self-aware admission of his constructed selfishness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Jeff acknowledges his selfishness while defending it. Self-aware but still selfish.

jokes.text: Is this a card? No, it isn't. Queen of Hearts. No, I'm asking, is this a card?
jokes.setup: Chang attempting a card trick
jokes.punchline: Professor's literal confusion about whether it's actually a card
jokes.explanation: Plays on Chang's incompetence and Professor's literal-mindedness. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure it's 'anus.'
jokes.setup: Professor saying 'e pluribus unum'
jokes.punchline: Chang's mishearing creating inappropriate word
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that fits Chang's character. Simple mishearing joke but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: And what we did was not dating.
jokes.setup: Jeff claiming no one has issues with Britta and Troy dating
jokes.punchline: Jeff's defensive correction about his and Britta's relationship
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's defensiveness about his past with Britta. Creates awkward moment but isn't particularly witty.

jokes.text: If there was heat, I'd be nervous, and, if I was nervous, I'd be rambling, and I'm definitely not rambling, so there's no heat.
jokes.setup: Britta denying there's romantic tension
jokes.punchline: Her obvious rambling while denying she's rambling
jokes.explanation: Classic self-defeating argument. Britta proves the opposite of what she's saying through her delivery. Well-structured irony.

jokes.text: That's a lie they tell dumb people when they're fitting them for work boots.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying grades don't matter outside school
jokes.punchline: Annie's brutal assessment of that philosophy
jokes.explanation: Savage and specific burn that reveals class anxiety. Perfectly captures Annie's academic intensity and fear of blue-collar fate.

jokes.text: I have Changnesia. I'm not a dog.
jokes.setup: Being told to calm down
jokes.punchline: Chang's indignant correction about his fake condition
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Chang's fake condition. The dignity he demands while maintaining his absurd premise is funny.

jokes.text: Okay, this is way better than Die Hard in a restaurant.
jokes.setup: The situation escalating with Professor tied up
jokes.punchline: Abed comparing real drama to his earlier entertainment wish
jokes.explanation: Good Abed moment comparing real situation to entertainment. Shows his detachment and callback skills.

jokes.text: And now, thanks to Kevin's complete misreading of the situation, we have our moment of maximum leverage
jokes.setup: Chang tying up Professor
jokes.punchline: Jeff spinning Chang's mistake as strategic advantage
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - turning disaster into opportunity through lawyer-speak. Good character moment showing his adaptability.

jokes.text: We'd all heard about your troubles with co-eds back at Oxford, but it was becoming all too real. As you lumbered around the room, we had to assume you were coming after me or Britta. Ah-ah-ahem! Or Shirley,
jokes.setup: Jeff creating false narrative about Professor
jokes.punchline: Having to awkwardly include Shirley as potential victim
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's improvised lawyering with awkward correction. The 'ahem' moment creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: who I didn't mention earlier, because she intimidates you sexually.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why he didn't mention Shirley initially
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous explanation about sexual intimidation
jokes.explanation: Jeff's absurd improvised explanation. Shows his ability to spin anything into legal-sounding argument.

jokes.text: This is amazing and possibly all in real time.
jokes.setup: The escalating situation with Professor offering deals
jokes.punchline: Abed noting the real-time dramatic structure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-observation. Acknowledges the bottle episode format while staying in character. Highly economical.

jokes.text: Promise not to monologue till I get back?
jokes.setup: Abed leaving to get popcorn during villain speech
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging villain monologue trope
jokes.explanation: Brilliant genre awareness. Abed recognizes and calls out the dramatic convention while participating in it. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: And what we did was not dating. Uh. Ah. A little friction.
jokes.setup: Jeff defensively correcting about dating Britta
jokes.punchline: Professor noting the obvious tension
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's defensiveness backfiring. Creates awkward moment but isn't particularly witty.

jokes.text: First of all, it's me, and, second of all, you put Britta ahead of me?
jokes.setup: Group guessing who might be valedictorian
jokes.punchline: Shirley's competitive outrage at being ranked below Britta
jokes.explanation: Reveals Shirley's hidden competitiveness and academic pride. Good character revelation with proper outrage structure.

jokes.text: Not this guy.
jokes.setup: Professor suggesting people hate Annie for the party disaster
jokes.punchline: Troy's simple but firm defense
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's loyalty and simple moral compass. Not clever but character-appropriate and heartwarming.

jokes.text: Tom Waits was... Ah! Ow. You cost me Valedictorian. He cost me Valedictorian. That was my punch.
jokes.setup: Jeff revealing he tanked his section
jokes.punchline: Annie and Shirley both trying to punch him, arguing over punch ownership
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with competitive element. Both women's academic ambitions create shared anger and shared violence.

jokes.text: Next time you decide to have a party without me, Jeffrey, at least have the decency to do it when I'm in spin class.
jokes.setup: Neighbor complaining about not being invited
jokes.punchline: Specific scheduling request for future exclusions
jokes.explanation: Good neighbor comedy. The specificity of 'spin class' makes the complaint funnier and more realistic.

jokes.text: I'm a blast at holiday-themed parties.
jokes.setup: Neighbor being excluded from Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Her self-promotional claim about party skills
jokes.explanation: Shows neighbor's desperation and self-promotion. Mildly funny but predictable lonely neighbor trope.

jokes.text: Now, one of you untied me. I wonder who it was. Who is the Benedict Arnold, the true hero of this story?
jokes.setup: Professor being untied by someone
jokes.punchline: Calling betrayer both traitor and hero
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference that fits Professor's character. The contradiction creates appropriate villain smugness.

jokes.text: Now I'm gonna do something that Octavian never would... yeah, I read the book.
jokes.setup: Jeff making historical comparison
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging his unexpected academic effort
jokes.explanation: Shows Jeff's growth and unexpected learning. The aside acknowledges his character development while staying true to his voice.

jokes.text: Once again, I'm not a dog.
jokes.setup: Being praised for wrapping presents
jokes.punchline: Chang's repeated insistence on human dignity
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Chang's dignity while maintaining his fake memory loss. Character-consistent but repetitive.

jokes.text: So you go... Make a little rabbit ear like so, then the kids go marching down the well.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining how to tie knots
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical but confident knot-tying instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - confident incompetence with child-like imagery. The nonsensical instructions reveal the fake knots.

jokes.text: Professor, you were never tied up.
jokes.setup: Chang's knot-tying explanation
jokes.punchline: Realization that Professor was never actually restrained
jokes.explanation: Brilliant plot twist that recontextualizes entire episode. Perfect setup and payoff with Chang's fake knot skills revealing the deception.

jokes.text: That I did it because I'm lonely? That my daughter isn't in town? That she never comes to see me because she finds me too manipulative?
jokes.setup: Being asked what he wants them to say
jokes.punchline: His cascade of increasingly sad personal revelations
jokes.explanation: Good escalating revelation of Professor's pathetic nature. Shows vulnerability behind his manipulation while maintaining consequences for his behavior.

jokes.text: Seeing as you saved me from my yearly Christmas tradition of plum pudding, roasted goose, and updating my suicide note...
jokes.setup: Professor explaining why he'll give them a better grade
jokes.punchline: His casually mentioned annual suicide note update
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation. Combines traditional Christmas imagery with suicide note in casual list format. Brilliant economy and shock value.

jokes.text: No, stupid, a gift doesn't create an obligation. It's the obligation that's a gift.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying gifts create obligations
jokes.punchline: Annie's deeper insight about the nature of caring
jokes.explanation: Beautiful callback and philosophical insight. Shows Annie's emotional intelligence and genuine understanding of community. Perfect character growth.

jokes.text: I gotta drop a deuce. See you guys.
jokes.setup: Heartwarming gift exchange moment
jokes.punchline: Chang's crude interruption of sentimental mood
jokes.explanation: Pure Chang - disrupting sentiment with crude humor. Character-appropriate but low-effort bathroom humor.

jokes.text: At nice times like this, I wonder what's happening in the darkest timeline.
jokes.setup: Happy moment with the group
jokes.punchline: Abed referencing show's alternate timeline concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta-moment referencing show mythology. Sets up the dark timeline sequence while staying true to character.

jokes.text: My client miss Edison did rob several drug stores, and, yes, she did stab several pharmacists, but let's talk about the bigger crime, that someone so beautiful has been removed from society.
jokes.setup: Dark timeline Jeff as lawyer
jokes.punchline: His shallow defense of violent criminal based on looks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark timeline humor showing evil Jeff's shallow priorities. Perfect escalation from robbery to stabbing to beauty defense.

jokes.text: Are you sure you don't have a problem with our age difference? Yeah, I wish you were even younger.
jokes.setup: Dark timeline Annie asking about age difference
jokes.punchline: Evil Jeff's disturbing response
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing how evil timeline corrupts normal relationship dynamics. Appropriately disturbing for the darker version.
